title: Amauropelta ctenitoides
intro: Amauropelta ctenitoides, also known as the Ctenitis fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Amauropelta ctenitoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Amauropelta ctenitoides is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Amauropelta ctenitoides is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This fern belongs to the genus Amauropelta. It is further classified as a member of the order Polypodiales. The family Thelypteridaceae is part of the subclass Polypodiidae. The class to which Amauropelta ctenitoides belongs is Pteridophyta.
